title: The Day Of Atonement Evangelion 10th Anniversary
display_title: The Day Of Atonement Evangelion 10th Anniversary
wikitext:
[]: The Day Of Atonement (Evangelion 10th Anniversary)             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Tue Oct 04, 2005 4:30 am 0230 Pacific Timeâ€”sixteen hours ahead in Japan. Ten years ago this night on Tokyo Channel 12; the sets came on. "There shall be a day of atonement: it shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall afflict your souls, and offer an offering made by fire." "I donâ€™t know what the result will be." â€”Hideaki Anno

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 04, 2005 5:51 am Cool - the 38th anniversary of Sputnik for the first showing of Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Oct 04, 2005 8:39 am Happy Birthday Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Tue Oct 04, 2005 10:21 am crazy....you people need to sleep.

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Tue Oct 04, 2005 10:28 am Actually, I hate the term "EVA" for Neon Genesis Evangelion, NGE or Neon Genesis Evangelion is the real thing! I hate evangelion, eva, whatever... Blerghk! Happy Birthday "Neon Genesis Evangelion"!

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Tue Oct 04, 2005 11:21 am yay!!!!!!!!!!! he's telling us to burn things! all must listen and uphold the teachings of the mighty Anno-sensie! happy birthday, you super fly mofo. with any luck, in a years time a couple more billion people will be saying the same (or at least simular) thing. i.e. live action causing eva awareness

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Oct 04, 2005 11:42 am Today is also the 47th anniversary of the Fifth French Republic. Vive La Evangelion! Vive La Republic!

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Tue Oct 04, 2005 1:09 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Today is also the 47th anniversary of the Fifth French Republic. Vive La Evangelion! Vive La Republic!

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Tue Oct 04, 2005 1:47 pm bp32 wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Today is also the 47th anniversary of the Fifth French Republic. Vive La Evangelion! Vive La Republic!

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Tue Oct 04, 2005 1:54 pm Carl Horn wrote: Hey, Gendo sounds superfly in his French dub. And VIVE LE GÃ‰NÃ‰RAL DE GAULLE ! VIVE LA RÃ‰PUBLIQUE ! VIVE LA FRANCE! Did you know that when the episode of THE PRISONER ("Misato, you told me, 'You are Number One.'"â€”Shinji, Episode #16) with the supercomputer called "The General" aired in France, they changed the name? A French writer noted, "Coincidences of that sort were not allowed in the France of Charles de Gaulle." â€”C.

[]:             Quote Post by The Sandman » Tue Oct 04, 2005 3:11 pm Here's a beer tost to Eva. Cheers.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Oct 04, 2005 8:57 pm Carl Horn wrote: Did you know that when the episode of THE PRISONER ("Misato, you told me, 'You are Number One.'"â€”Shinji, Episode #16) with the supercomputer called "The General" aired in France, they changed the name? A French writer noted, "Coincidences of that sort were not allowed in the France of Charles de Gaulle."

[]:             Quote Post by geishaboy » Tue Oct 04, 2005 10:30 pm Evangelion has aged quite nicely I must say. Like fine vintage wine that gives you a nice buzz when you take that first sip, and a massive headache when you finish the bottle.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Oct 04, 2005 10:37 pm Eva is a good veteran (both senses). So, at what times exactly did Eva air on TV? I thought there was one slot at something around 19, and one late at night, whose time don't know. But I also read somewhere that Eva became popular in the re-run rather than on release. Is that true?

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Wed Oct 05, 2005 2:51 pm AchtungAffen wrote: Eva is a good veteran (both senses). So, at what times exactly did Eva air on TV? I thought there was one slot at something around 19, and one late at night, whose time don't know. But I also read somewhere that Eva became popular in the re-run rather than on release. Is that true?

[]:             Quote Post by BLACKANGEL32076 » Tue Oct 11, 2005 11:54 pm Happy Belated Birthday Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Oct 12, 2005 12:00 am Some how I think Anno trys to forget this date evey year . .

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Oct 12, 2005 2:21 pm DatDude wrote: Some how I think Anno trys to forget this date evey year . .

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Oct 12, 2005 2:28 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: DatDude wrote: Some how I think Anno trys to forget this date evey year . .

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Wed Oct 12, 2005 3:49 pm Er...guess I should say happy Birthday, too. Belated. Wasn't it aired on Rosh Hashana...and the anniversary and all was on Rosh Hashana this year? Yah, 'cause I didn't go to temple this year for the first time in my life. O.o Anno is mah luver.
